message:
Fix type safety and formatting in property-based tests

Addresses clippy warnings and applies formatting improvements to the proptest
suite for hash, ID generation, and timestamp parsing.

proptest_time.rs - Type safety fix:
- Replace unsafe "parsed.year() as u32" cast with explicit i32::try_from(year)
  conversion. The year strategy generates u32 values in range 1970..=2100,
  and chrono's year() returns i32. The previous cast could silently truncate
  or wrap on edge cases. Now uses try_from with expect() to make the
  assumption explicit and fail fast if violated.
- Apply inline format argument style to timestamp construction:
  format!("{year:04}-{month:02}-{day:02}T{hour:02}:{minute:02}:{second:02}Z")
  instead of format!("{:04}-{:02}-...", year, month, ...)

proptest_id.rs - Clippy lint fix:
- Import std::fmt::Write trait to use write! macro properly for string building
- Replace id.push_str(&format!(".{seg}")) with write!(id, ".{seg}") pattern
  This avoids allocating an intermediate String for each segment, addressing
  clippy::format_push_string lint (or similar) while being more idiomatic
- The let _ = write!() pattern explicitly ignores the Result since writing
  to a String is infallible

proptest_hash.rs - Formatting:
- Consolidate multi-line info! macro call to single line where it fits
  within line limits, improving visual consistency

No behavioral changes; all property tests maintain their invariants.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
